title: Hash regex delimiter is optional, allowing false substring matches
description:
## Location
scripts/analyze-hetero-fleet.py:61

## Description
The pattern `hash[=:]?\s*` makes the = or : delimiter optional (? means 0 or 1). This means 'hashABCDEF01' would match 'ABCDEF01' even without a delimiter. Words containing 'hash' followed by hex could produce false matches.

## Failure Scenario
A debug log contains 'rehashABCDEF01234567 operation complete'. The regex matches 'ABCDEF01234567' as a hash because 'hash' is a substring and no delimiter is required. This spurious hash pollutes the TX set and will never be received, inflating the missing packet count.

## Category
parsing
